mission: Make hosted stays reliable enough to be booked repeatedly by guests and simple enough to be run profitably by hosts, co-hosts, and professional operators through one marketplace with strong trust, pricing, payment, and protection controls.

businessModel: Guest and host service fees on booked stays
Higher utilization of active stay inventory
Repeat guest demand with app-led retention
Higher-quality professional supply and co-host-enabled operations
Payments, refund, and support cost discipline
marketForces: Guests increasingly compare hosted stays against hotels on trust, clarity, and convenience, not only uniqueness
Hosts want marketplace demand without becoming full-time revenue managers or support agents
Professional operators expect portfolio-grade tools, permissions, and reconciliation
Cities and regulators continue to shape listing eligibility, permit logic, and operating restrictions
Trust, identity, and issue recovery quality increasingly determine both supply retention and demand conversion
keyPartners: Professional hosts, managers, and co-host operators
Payment processors and tax remittance partners
Local compliance, insurance, and support partners

products.name: Marketplace Trust, Payments, and Support Control Tower

designPrinciples: Keep guest conversion and host activation close enough that listing quality and trust loops stay connected.
Treat trust, payments, AirCover, and support as first-class product capabilities rather than background operations.
Split everyday host simplicity from professional operator depth while keeping one marketplace core.
Model compliance as reusable platform policy control rather than bespoke local exceptions everywhere.
Use shared marketplace intelligence to steer search, pricing, quality, and issue-cost tradeoffs.

recommendedStructure.mission: Own guest discovery, booking, trip confidence, and repeat demand.
Own host activation, listing quality, calendar and pricing control, and host earnings visibility.
Own co-hosting, portfolio operations, multi-listing control, and operator-grade workflows.
Own identity, risk, AirCover, support, payouts, tax flows, and market-rule enforcement.
Provide ranking, pricing, experimentation, APIs, and platform reliability foundations.

operatingModelNotes: The structural tension is balancing emotionally sensitive consumer trust with economically sensitive host control.
Hosted stays need stronger exception handling than many pure digital marketplaces because support failures directly affect physical travel.
The model scales only if pricing, trust, payouts, and compliance remain shared systems rather than fragmented by persona.
